title: Cyphostemma gallaense
taxonomy: Cyphostemma gallaense is classified in the family Vitaceae and belongs to the genus Cyphostemma. This classification places it within the order Rhamnales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the grapevine. The family Vitaceae is a group of woody vines and shrubs that are commonly known as the grape family. The genus Cyphostemma is a group of succulent plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa. Cyphostemma gallaense is a specific species within this classification, and further research is needed to fully understand its characteristics and classification.

intro: Cyphostemma gallaense, also known as the Galla grape, is a medicinal plant species native to the Horn of Africa, characterized by its succulent stems and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including hypertension, diabetes, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Cyphostemma gallaense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cyphostemma gallaense is often prepared in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
